howItWorks.intro: This long-term care calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Future Cost = Current Daily × (1 + inflation)^years until care
Total = Future Daily Cost × 365 × Years of Care
howItWorks.outro: Insurance rates depend on many individual factors. Get personalized quotes from licensed providers.

faq.question: How much does long-term care cost?
faq.answer: In 2026, the national median is: home health aide $30/hour, assisted living $5,350/month, nursing home $275/day ($8,365/month). Costs vary dramatically by state.

faq.question: How long does the average person need care?
faq.answer: Average is 3 years. Women average 3.7 years, men 2.2 years. About 15% of people need care for 5+ years. Planning for 3-5 years is prudent.

faq.question: When should I buy LTC insurance?
faq.answer: Ideal age is 55-65. Younger means lower premiums and better health qualification. After 70, premiums are very expensive and health issues may prevent qualification.

faq.question: What are alternatives to LTC insurance?
faq.answer: Self-insure with savings, hybrid life/LTC policies, Medicaid (requires spending down assets), family caregiving, or continuing care retirement communities (CCRCs).

faq.question: Is the Long-Term Care Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Long-Term Care Calculator?
sections.body: The Long-Term Care Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the insurance planning space. By entering your current age, daily care cost, expected years of care, you get instant results including future daily cost, total care cost, est. monthly premium. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting future daily cost right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In insurance planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to choose the right coverage with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your current age and need to find the right future daily cost. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Current Age
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your current age — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: daily care cost, expected years of care, care cost inflation. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your future daily cost, total care cost, est. monthly premium. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your current age before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both future daily cost and total care cost — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to choose the right coverage.
If you're unsure about your care cost inflation, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
